# Hey Sammy

> Hey Sammy is an iOS app that helps people find real-world local activities, understand how to show up, and build friendships through repeated participation.

Hey Sammy is for people who want to spend less time passively scrolling and more time doing things in real life. It focuses on recurring, participatory activities where a solo newcomer can become a regular over time: run clubs, dance socials, pickleball, pottery, language exchange, volunteering, hiking groups, board game nights, fitness classes, and similar local communities.

The core product promise is practical: Hey Sammy helps someone move from "I should get out more" to "I know where to go, when to go, what to expect, and what to do next."

## Primary Pages

- [Homepage](https://tryheysammy.com/index.html.md): Overview of the Hey Sammy app, product promise, App Store link, and social links.
- [Blog](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/index.html.md): Essays from Hey Sammy about friendship, third spaces, courage, recurring activities, and getting back into the real world.
- [Stop Waiting for an Epiphany](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/stop-waiting-for-an-epiphany/index.html.md): Essay on why clarity comes from trying real things instead of waiting for a perfect life plan.
- [The First-Timer Script](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/the-first-timer-script/index.html.md): Essay with a practical script for walking into a new activity alone and starting one small interaction.
- [Remote Work Needs Real-World Rituals](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/remote-work-needs-real-world-rituals/index.html.md): Essay on why remote workers need recurring real-world rituals that pull life back out of the house.
- [The Best Activities for Making Friends Have Two Things in Common](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/the-best-activities-for-making-friends/index.html.md): Essay on why participation and conversation make some recurring activities better for friendship.
- [Being Bad at Something Is Good for You](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/being-bad-at-something-is-good-for-you/index.html.md): Essay on why beginner courage and awkward first attempts make real-world connection easier.
- [Why Men Need Things to Do Together](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/why-men-need-things-to-do-together/index.html.md): Essay on why shared activities, repetition, and side-by-side participation make friendship easier for men.
- [Third Spaces Are Where Life Gets Bigger](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/third-spaces-are-where-life-gets-bigger/index.html.md): Essay on why a fuller life depends on recurring places with regulars and rituals.
- [The Life You Build by Showing Up](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/the-life-you-build-by-showing-up/index.html.md): Essay on how confidence follows action and a fuller life gets built by showing up.
- [You Don't Get Lucky Sitting at Home](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/you-dont-get-lucky-sitting-at-home/index.html.md): Founder note on trying things, showing up, and why Hey Sammy is built around recurring activities.
- [Why Familiar Faces Become Friends](https://tryheysammy.com/blog/why-familiar-faces-become-friends/index.html.md): Essay on why repeated exposure and familiar faces make friendship easier.

## App And Social Links

- [Download Hey Sammy on the App Store](https://apps.apple.com/us/app/hey-sammy-try-things-irl/id6766004631)
- [Hey Sammy on Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/heysammyapp/)
- [Hey Sammy on TikTok](https://www.tiktok.com/@heysammyapp)

## Entity Notes

- Brand name: Hey Sammy
- Company/copyright holder shown on the site: OMG Goals
- Site URL: https://tryheysammy.com/
- App category: lifestyle, local activities, social connection, real-world participation
- Current app focus: helping people find local plans and recurring activities that are worth leaving the house for

## Optional

- [Product context](https://tryheysammy.com/): Human-facing homepage with visuals and App Store CTA.
